verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a sperm-specific single-pass transmembrane protein belonging to the Ig-like cell adhesion molecule and IZUMO family. It resides intracellularly in non-capacitated sperm but undergoes a well-documented relocalisation to the sperm plasma membrane (outer leaflet) upon capacitation/acrosome reaction, where it mediates sperm-oocyte plasma membrane binding and fusion. This makes the protein surface-accessible only in the specialised germline/gamete context — a narrow tissue-restricted and cell-state-induced surface display. It is not broadly expressed on somatic cell surfaces. The anchor is a genuine TM domain in that compartment, but expression and surface display are restricted to mature sperm undergoing fertilisation, fitting tissue_restricted_surface under contextual.
